     The Numbers Business Report

Boogeyman (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $46,752,382Details
International Box Office $20,440,477
Worldwide Box Office $67,192,859
Further financial details...

Synopsis

As a young boy, Tim witnesses the horrifying event of his father being dragged away by the boogeyman. Despite the reassurances of his mother that the boogeyman is not real, he is never the same afterward. Years later, Tim manages to create a normal life for himself — though he lives without closets and keeps his mattress on the floor. But when he learns of his mother’s death, Tim must return home to face his childhood trauma.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$19,020,655 (40.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.46 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:69.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,052 opening theaters/3,052 max. theaters, 4.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $82,491,329

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 4th, 2005 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: May 13th, 2005 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: May 31st, 2005 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of horror and terror/violence, and some partial nudity.
Running Time: 89 minutes
Franchise: Boogeyman
Social setting: Supernatural
Subgenre: Supernatural Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Mandate Pictures
Production Countries: United States, New Zealand
Languages: English

Synopsis

As a young boy, Tim witnesses the horrifying event of his father being dragged away by the boogeyman. Despite the reassurances of his mother that the boogeyman is not real, he is never the same afterward. Years later, Tim manages to create a normal life for himself — though he lives without closets and keeps his mattress on the floor. But when he learns of his mother’s death, Tim must return home to face his childhood trauma.
